content:
Write a 200-word abstract for a systematic review answering: "What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting in adults with metabolic syndrome?"

Section summaries:
- Overview and Definitions: Caloric restriction (CR)5 and total caloric desistance [TCD; i.e., intermittent fasting, alternate-day fasting (ADF), routine periodic fasti... [2]. The idea is that it's easier to sharply restrict calories a few days a week or to limit eating to a shortened "eating window" each day than ... [1].

...
- Evidence Base and Efficacy: Johns Hopkins neuroscientist Mark Mattson, who has studied intermittent fasting for 25 years, explains this dietary approach. [5]. Subgroup analyses were performed for populations based on three specific status: diabetes, overweight or obesity, and metabolic syndrome. [4].

- John...
- Methodological Considerations: This review of 22 randomized controlled trials with a combined 1,995 participants examined several forms of intermittent fasting: time-restr... [6]. A small 2025 study found that overweight participants who practiced two months of intermittent fasting were more likely to maintain the patt... [7].

...
- Safety and Long-Term Evidence: To decrease some of these unwanted side effects, you may want to switch from adf fasting to periodic fasting or a time restricted eating pla... [1]. [2] [1] [5]
